topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air bit at Elias’s exposed skin as he stood by the locker room door, the weight of the rejection settling like a physical stone in his chest. He stared at the team roster posted on the corkboard, the names he’d poured sweat and early mornings into training alongside, now conspicuously absent from the final lineup. His hands, usually steady when gripping a barbell, trembled slightly as he ran a thumb over the faded lettering of his jersey. "Maybe I just wasn't fast enough," he muttered to himself, the sound thin and reedy in the echoing space. He felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to retreat, to simply disappear into the shadows of the hallway, the noise of the team’s camaraderie suddenly sounding like a mocking chorus.

[story 2]

“So, that’s it?” Maya asked, her voice barely above a whisper, her eyes fixed on the ground. She traced the condensation on her water bottle, the plastic cool against her fingertips. The silence in the room was heavy, thick with unspoken disappointment. She felt a strange lightness, a sort of hollowness where fierce determination used to reside. When the coach finally called the names, her stomach clenched, a tight, nauseating knot forming just beneath her ribs. She managed a small, tight nod, forcing a smile that felt brittle on her face, acutely aware of how much effort it took just to keep her posture upright.

[story 3]

I kept replaying the final drill in my head, the missed pass, the hesitation that had cost me the spot. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, unresponsive. When the coach announced the final roster, the world seemed to tilt. I didn't move immediately; I just stood there, my gaze locked on the polished floor, watching dust motes dance in the harsh overhead lights. A prickling sensation started at the base of my neck, spreading outwards, making my skin crawl. When the coach looked my way, his expression was neutral, and that blankness was worse than any harsh criticism. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my throat making the action painful, and mumbled a quick, almost inaudible "Understood."
